commands.review_summary:
The PR adds an experimental Apple Silicon Virtualization.framework provider, a separate helper binary, provider configuration and documentation, lifecycle hardening, and unit and smoke-script coverage.

Reproducibility: yes. for the distribution defect: the proposed release configuration packages only `crabbox`, while the registered provider requires `crabbox-apple-vz-helper`. The runtime hardening claims cannot be reproduced with high confidence from the supplied evidence because no real Apple Silicon run is shown.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Maintainers must choose the official helper-distribution and remote-image trust contracts, after which the contributor should repair the branch and supply real-device proof.

target.title: Add and harden Apple VZ provider
target.body:
Fixes #262.

## Summary

This PR adds the Apple VZ provider work and hardens the highest-value startup/runtime failures found before submission.

The follow-up fix commit does four things:

- keeps ownership of the spawned helper during `runStart` until readiness is known, then cleans up the helper process and instance directory on readiness timeout
- handles `stopping` / `stopped` readiness metadata as a specific stopped-before-readiness path instead of reporting a misleading helper crash
- treats `VirtualMachineStateError` and local SSH proxy failures as terminal runtime errors that persist `StatusError` and request VM stop
- preserves explicit top-level `workRoot` / `CRABBOX_WORK_ROOT` unless `appleVZ.workRoot` is specifically configured, and passes `BootstrapWaitTimeout(cfg)` into helper `start --ready-timeout`

## Why

Without these changes, common Apple VZ startup and runtime failures can leave local helper daemons, VM state, or metadata behind. The most visible case is a slow first boot: the helper could time out before the provider's intended bootstrap budget elapsed, then return without tearing down the local VM/helper state.

## Verification

Verified locally on the rebased branch with these commands:

```sh
GOCACHE=/tmp/crabbox-go-cache go test ./...
node --test scripts/live-smoke.test.js
GOCACHE=/tmp/crabbox-go-cache go vet ./internal/applevzhelper ./internal/providers/applevz ./internal/cli
GOCACHE=/tmp/crabbox-go-cache go test -race ./internal/applevzhelper ./internal/providers/applevz
```

Results:

- `go test ./...`: passed across the Go packages, including `cmd/crabbox`, `internal/cli`, `internal/applevzhelper`, `internal/providers/applevz`, `internal/providers/all`, and the current provider packages on `main`.
- `node --test scripts/live-smoke.test.js`: passed 11/11 tests, including Apple VZ and Morph live-smoke script coverage.
- `go vet ./internal/applevzhelper ./internal/providers/applevz ./internal/cli`: passed with exit code 0.
- `go test -race ./internal/applevzhelper ./internal/providers/applevz`: passed with exit code 0.

## Notes

I intentionally kept lower-value edge cases out of this PR pass. The landed changes focus on typical Apple VZ startup, first-boot, runtime-error, proxy-error, and work-root override behavior.
target.author: coygeek
